recommendations.condition: Visible roof deck sagging and water damage indicating potential structural compromise
recommendations.action: Engage structural engineer for immediate assessment; restrict access to affected area pending evaluation

recommendations.condition: Electrical panel clearance obstructed by stored materials
recommendations.action: Maintain minimum 36-inch clearance in front of all electrical panels per NEC Article 110.26

recommendations.condition: Hood suppression system in kitchen area has expired inspection tag
recommendations.action: Schedule semi-annual inspection of kitchen hood suppression system per NFPA 96 Section 12.2

recommendations.condition: Exterior lighting inadequate in parking area and loading dock
recommendations.action: Install additional lighting to maintain minimum 1 foot-candle at ground level in all exterior areas

recommendations.condition: HVAC rooftop units show signs of deferred maintenance and corrosion
recommendations.action: Establish quarterly preventive maintenance program for all mechanical equipment

recommendations.condition: Hot work permit program not formally documented
recommendations.action: Implement written hot work permit program per NFPA 51B; train all affected personnel

items: [Code] Multiple areas of ponding water observed on flat roof sections
[Code] Multiple areas of ponding water observed on flat roof sections

deficiencies: [Code] Multiple areas of ponding water observed on flat roof sections

raw_ground_truth.loss_run_label: Consolidated Loss Summary (prepared by Capitol Insurance Advisors)

description.value: Employee’s suffered a laceration to the left hand while opening a commercial-size can of tomatoes with a wall-mounted opener.
Employee’s experienced a’laceration to the right forearm while cleaning the meat slicer blade after closing.

denial_reason.value: Pre-existing condition unrelated to work activity

description.value: Employee developed de Quervain's tenosynovitis to the​​​​​​​​​​​ right wrist while performing repetitive plating motions during service.

Employee’s experienced an Achilles tendon rupture to the left ankle while slipping on ice in the company parking lot during early morning hours. Surgery was required to repair the damage, followed by 8 weeks of rehabilitation.

Employee suffered a labral tear and impingement to the​​​​​​​​​​​ left hip while tripping over equipment left in the walkway. The injury…………………………… required a splint and modified work duties for 4 weeks.
Employee suffered a meniscus tear to the left knee while twisting while exiting a company vehicle. Treatment included physical therapy and a course of anti-inflammatory medication.
Employee sustained multiple rib contusions to the​​​​​​​​​​​ chest while falling against a steel railing during a slip. The injury required a splint and modified work duties for 4 weeks.
Employee experienced a’syndesmosis injury…………………………… to the right ankle while landing awkwardly after jumping from equipment.
Employee suffered a’fracture of the lateral malleolus to the left ankle while slipping on ice in the company parking lot during early morning hours.
Employee sustained a severe contusion to…
Employee’s sustained a severe sprain to...
Guest at the hotel was burned by...
Guest at the hotel was burned…
A vendor fell down a stairwell when the handrail…

merged_regions.value: Values should be entered as whole dollar amounts.
Construction codes: 1=Frame, 2=Joisted Masonry, 3=Non-Combustible, 4=Masonry Non-Combustible, 5=Modified Fire Resistive, 6=Fire Resistive

nature_of_business.value: Boutique hotel ownership and management with integrated restaurant and event operations across Central and South Texas

risk_management.question: Are short-term rentals (less than 1 year) permitted?
Is there 24-hour doorperson or security on premises?
What percentage of units are regularly occupied vs. vacant?
Is there any commercial occupancy within the building?
Are there swimming pools, exercise rooms, or other amenities?
When was the last plumbing, HVAC, and electrical system update?
risk_management.answer: HVAC replaced 2020; plumbing and electrical original (2005)

risk_management.question: Are written contracts with indemnification required from all contractors?
Are EV charging stations installed on the premises?
Is the building fully sprinklered per NFPA 13 or 13R?
Are fire alarms hard-wired with central station monitoring?
Is there a quality control / quality assurance program?
Are Material Safety Data Sheets (MSDS) maintained?
Have there been any OSHA citations in the past 3 years?
Are cyber liability protections in place?
